title: Random questions NGE Rewatch
wikitext:
[]: Random questions (NGE Rewatch)             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 11, 2011 5:14 pm This is a general questions thread on whatever pops into my mind. I did glance at the wiki as needed but I'm still a bit puzzled. Maybe I'm having a memory problem with not being able to keep all this shit straight, but it is a LOT to take in. (Or perhaps NME is making me forgot my NGE knowledge. >_> ) Putting this in Chit-Chat instead of Discussion because I don't necessarily except this thread to be completely serious business; mostly casual discussion about this stuff. ~Ritsuko's Findings in Episode 05: Shouldn't she have already known that Angels and Humans aren't far apart considering her higher-up knowledge (similar to Gendo and Fuyu) of Adam/Lilith/etc? Or is Ritsuko really not in the know about like her two superiors, only certain matters? I know that scene in general was a plot point for the viewers to keep in mind, but it's starting to strike me as a little weird. Unless there's something obvious I'm missing here. ~In a nutshell, the Committee is the official U.N. front for Seele while Seele itself is the "behind the scenes" part? This has always confused me. ~The guy on the plane with Gendo in Episode 07: Is this Chinese(??) man part of the Committee or Seele? I find the talk of his country having plans for Eva-08 to be interesting, but I'm not sure if that talk of future Eva units is early reference to the Mass-Production Eva units, or if they were meant to be Eva units proper themselves. IIRC, Episode 22' or so revealed that Eva-06 and I think 05 were scrap parts for something or another and there was a hint of MP Eva construction happening... ~Guy on the phone at the start of Episode 07: As far as we know, none of the supplemental materials say whether it's just Kaji's seiyuu or supposed to BE Kaji, yes? Considering the man's "Then I'll continue as planned" comment, I personally think it is supposed to be Kaji (just before he and Asuka are en route to Japan) but...yeah. ~Whether an Angel would have made contact with the Adam embryo or contacting Lilith in Terminal Dogma, WOULD Third Impact have indeed occurred? Episode 19 says this is the case, but I guess I'm confused on the matter because neither Lilith or Adam are reunited with their respective souls until EoE... Rei's soul is split between Rei 2 and Eva-00, while Kaworu's apparently off in Germany (I guess?) somewhere, so what would the Angels have to contact, really? In Adam's case, perhaps merging with Adam causes the Angel to become Adam, but I'm not sure what would happen with Lilith except for Angel pokes Lilith, BOOM!, Life is reset/everybody's tanged. And maybe that is that's all there would be to it? The Eva2 game makes a point of Rei being drawn to Lilith (and merging with Lilith) if an Angel reaches Terminal Dogma, which is a nice touch, but... ~I'm sure this was asked before, but considering Rei's problems with Eva-00, why didn't Nerv HQ send for Asuka and Eva-02 sooner? Of course, Gendo needed Shinji inside Eva-01/Yui as part of his plans (pretty sure Eva-01 gaining the S2 Engine was on his Third Impact Checklist), but wouldn't it smarter for them to have more backup, especially considering Shinji was completely inexperienced whereas Rei and Asuka have had formal training with Eva? Plot-wise, it makes sense for Shinji being solo with piloting for awhile and in general him getting used to his surroundings, but in-universe-wise, it doesn't seem very smart on Nerv's part. The least they could have done was send for Shinji sooner to train him, but I guess Nerv had no idea when the Angels would appear versus not, unless the DSS say something about that. ~On a similar line of thought, wouldn't it have been better on the "Marduk Institute" (IT'S GENDO!) to find and train the Fourth Children ASAP? Of course, they'd need an Eva unit to go along with the Children, but why not work on a "backup" Eva/potential pilot on the off-chance an Angel really injures/screws over the active pilots?

[]:             Quote Post by LordEvan88 » Mon Apr 11, 2011 6:00 pm ~ I figure the Adam sample is in the possession of Seele and is being kept from Ritsuko as a reference. As for Lilith, well, its obvious she is more similar to humans than Adam or the Angels. Its a stretch, but I can understand her surprise. ~No comment on Seele, they appear to have as many faces as they do eyes ~I figure they were meant to be proper Eva units, but I imagine as they were constructed Seele was continually sneaking parts off them for use in the construction of the series. (Although theoretically, the synthesis of an S2 engine from the 4th angel data may have rapidly accelerated the 'growth' of the Eva series, allowing their rapid construction) ~Guessing Kaji ~I'm still sketchy on this myself. I would advise consulting the wiki regarding Lilith and Adam. Although I am curious if the destruction of Unit 00 resulted in the re fusion of Rei/Lilith's soul. The distinction of soul/physical contact is an element I have just become aware of and I am interested if anyone has further insight. ~I am curious the variables put into Marduk selection. Beyond Rei/Kaworu and contact experiments involved I don't know who would be applicable candidates. What 'soul' was implanted into Unit 03+? Basically, I imagine backups are useful but I don't know what children can sync with Evas and which can't and how you know the difference.

[]: Re: Random questions (NGE Rewatch)             Quote Post by Jessemon » Mon Apr 11, 2011 6:12 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: This is a general questions thread on whatever pops into my mind. I did glance at the wiki as needed but I'm still a bit puzzled. Maybe I'm having a memory problem with not being able to keep all this shit straight, but it is a LOT to take in. (Or perhaps NME is making me forgot my NGE knowledge. >_> ) Putting this in Chit-Chat instead of Discussion because I don't necessarily except this thread to be completely serious business; mostly casual discussion about this stuff. ~Ritsuko's Findings in Episode 05: Shouldn't she have already known that Angels and Humans aren't far apart considering her higher-up knowledge (similar to Gendo and Fuyu) of Adam/Lilith/etc? Or is Ritsuko really not in the know about like her two superiors, only certain matters? I know that scene in general was a plot point for the viewers to keep in mind, but it's starting to strike me as a little weird. Unless there's something obvious I'm missing here. Whether or not she should have known is really ambiguous. She would have been Misato's age during 2nd impact, so she wouldn't have directly seen any related data on Adam; however theoretically that data would have been saved and stored. Then again, scientists at the time may not have been able to get a DNA sample from Adam. So really, idk. >_> ~In a nutshell, the Committee is the official U.N. front for Seele while Seele itself is the "behind the scenes" part? This has always confused me. Sounds right. ~The guy on the plane with Gendo in Episode 07: Is this Chinese(??) man part of the Committee or Seele? I find the talk of his country having plans for Eva-08 to be interesting, but I'm not sure if that talk of future Eva units is early reference to the Mass-Production Eva units, or if they were meant to be Eva units proper themselves. IIRC, Episode 22' or so revealed that Eva-06 and I think 05 were scrap parts for something or another and there was a hint of MP Eva construction happening... I believe at that point China would probably have been constructing a "proper" Eva. The MP Eva's were most likely all constructed in the same place, and didn't seem to be related to any country; just Seele itself. ~Whether an Angel would have made contact with the Adam embryo or contacting Lilith in Terminal Dogma, WOULD Third Impact have indeed occurred? Episode 19 says this is the case, but I guess I'm confused on the matter because neither Lilith or Adam are reunited with their respective souls until EoE... Rei's soul is split between Rei 2 and Eva-00, while Kaworu's apparently off in Germany (I guess?) somewhere, so what would the Angels have to contact, really? In Adam's case, perhaps merging with Adam causes the Angel to become Adam, but I'm not sure what would happen with Lilith except for Angel pokes Lilith, BOOM!, Life is reset/everybody's tanged. And maybe that is that's all there would be to it? The Eva2 game makes a point of Rei being drawn to Lilith (and merging with Lilith) if an Angel reaches Terminal Dogma, which is a nice touch, but... Again, who knows. The major problem here is that the answer itself probably changed several times throughout the series. In hindsight, it SEEMS that the only real way 3I can occur is if Lilith or Adam had their respective souls returned to them and if either one was touched by another angel, because that is what both Seele and Gendo attempted to do (and is ultimately what happened in a roundabout fashion). Any statements to the contrary throughout the series can then be chalked up to ignorance on the part of the character or deliberate misdirection. ~I'm sure this was asked before, but considering Rei's problems with Eva-00, why didn't Nerv HQ send for Asuka and Eva-02 sooner? Of course, Gendo needed Shinji inside Eva-01/Yui as part of his plans (pretty sure Eva-01 gaining the S2 Engine was on his Third Impact Checklist), but wouldn't it smarter for them to have more backup, especially considering Shinji was completely inexperienced whereas Rei and Asuka have had formal training with Eva? Plot-wise, it makes sense for Shinji being solo with piloting for awhile and in general him getting used to his surroundings, but in-universe-wise, it doesn't seem very smart on Nerv's part. The least they could have done was send for Shinji sooner to train him, but I guess Nerv had no idea when the Angels would appear versus not, unless the DSS say something about that. Who knows. They did transport Unit 02 an Asuka by ship from Germany, but that shouldn't take more than what, a couple weeks? Maybe 3? The timeline is sketchy between the first episode and when we see Asuka, but I'm 90% sure that Gendo says within the first couple episodes that Rei will be ready to fight in something like 22 days, and she is back up and running before Asuka arrives, so.... ~On a similar line of thought, wouldn't it have been better on the "Marduk Institute" (IT'S GENDO!) to find and train the Fourth Children ASAP? Of course, they'd need an Eva unit to go along with the Children, but why not work on a "backup" Eva/potential pilot on the off-chance an Angel really injures/screws over the active pilots? Well, without an Eva, there would have been no point. As far as we know, a 4th pilot wouldn't be able to operate any of the other Evas.

[]: Re: Random questions (NGE Rewatch)             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Apr 12, 2011 2:46 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: ~Ritsuko's Findings in Episode 05:

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Apr 12, 2011 10:16 am All these answers make sense to me. I knew I was brain-farting on certain things! If people want to ask their own questions in here or still reply with their own two cents, they can go ahead, I don't mind.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 14, 2011 11:58 am Question: So what's with Kaji having another version of his goodbye message to Misato? Did he leave two on Misato's answering machine or something? 21 and 23 wrote: Kaji (ANSWERING MACHINE): Katsuragi, it's me. I've probably put you through a lot of trouble by the time you're listening to this. I'm sorry. Tell Ritchan I said I'm sorry too. Also, since I've troubled you so much anyway, there's this flower I've been growing... It'd make me happy if you'd water it for me. Shinji knows where it is. Katsuragi, the truth is with you. Go forward without any hesitation. If I ever get to see you again, I'll say the words I wasn't able to say eight years ago. Bye.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Apr 14, 2011 12:16 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: versus Near the end of 23 wrote: Kaji (VOICE RECORDING): This is a part of the truth that you wanted. I devised thirty-six other ways to send it to you, but you probably won't get them. The only definite one is this capsule. This is everything I have to offer. Do with it as you see fit. The password is our very first memory together. Bye, take care.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Apr 14, 2011 1:32 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Also: What the hell is Rei 3 synching with in Episode 24 during the tests?!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 14, 2011 2:25 pm Sachi and Tines : Thanks! That makes sense. This has been bugging me for some time, and I don't recall seeing a thread for it anywhere. From Episode 26: What the heck is the desk (Asuka's? Shinji's?) saying besides the "Anta baka"? Here it is upside if that makes it easier to view: The second part looks like Shinji's last name (Ikari 碇 ) but the first doesn't really look like Soryu ( 惣流 ...unless the person's handwriting is really that crappy) so... Yeah, I'm lost. I'm assuming it is some kind of "love love umbrella" http://japan-cc.com/umbrella.htm reference (and Childhood-friend!Asuka's all pissy about it), but my kanji sucks too much for me to figure it out.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 14, 2011 3:14 pm 明日香 = Asuka. Guess it would take an alternate universe for the characters' names to be written in kanji. There's also "iyaaaaa na kanji" (one of Kensuke's lines) written above it. The simulation bodies, BTW, just acted as proxies to the pilots' respective Evas. I don't think Rei was synching with one of those.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 14, 2011 3:23 pm So it's a Shinji/Asuka love-love umbrella? Naisu! No wonder the GoS2 game/manga came into existence. I wonder if Toji or Kensuke wrote that to get AU!Asuka and AU!Shinji going ("We're just childhood friends!"), hehe. Bizarre that ADV didn't translate the rest of the desk, but it's similar to them not translating the German dialogue of Asuka's phonecall (but it's in Platinum booklet) or the German text of Arael attacking Eva-02 (before we get into the Inner Space Mindrape sequence, where the German text is translated)... And yeah, funny how Anno(?) decided to keep the character names written in katakana. I'm surprised I brainfarted on that being Asuka's name in Kanji. And thanks for clearing up stuff about the plug bodies.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:45 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Here it is upside if that makes it easier to view: The second part looks like Shinji's last name (Ikari 碇 ) but the first doesn't really look like Soryu ( 惣流 ...unless the person's handwriting is really that crappy) so... Yeah, I'm lost. I'm assuming it is some kind of "love love umbrella" http://japan-cc.com/umbrella.htm reference (and Childhood-friend!Asuka's all pissy about it), but my kanji sucks too much for me to figure it out.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:48 pm Toji actually drew heart marks? The Toji (husband and wife) /Kensuke (That's suspicious!)/Asuka (ANTA BAKA?!) commentary is hilarious, why didn't ADV Films translate that?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 15, 2011 12:10 pm I just noticed something weird(?) with the DC Episode 21' start. So, we have the scientists talking about the Contact Experiment with Adam in the next month/Gendo and Kiel mocking them in the background. All of a sudden, there's footage of Adam walking around. This was a rather jarring cut for me, but I guess it's supposed to be some sudden passage of time/jumping to Second Impact? Should the video footage of reflected that, or was somehow reflected and I just missed it? Basically the cuts jump seamlessly from this: to this: (Image sauces: http://evacommentary.org/episode-21/epi ... cene1.html ) Which isn't such a bad thing, but it make it appear as if Second Impact is happening all of a sudden with Gendo and Kiel right there (and they high-tailed it out of there a month before 2I). ___________ Same episode, another bit of confusion. Gendo is the one who proposed HIP (and I guess by extension, 3I?) to Kiel/Seele, yes? So what's with this exchange between Fuyutsuki and Gendo? This is long before Yui or Naoko kick the bucket after all. Unless the exchange somehow relates to Gendo knowing the Angels would reappear at some point, but what does the implication of Third Impact have to do with anything? This makes my head hurt. First, they start with the talk of the Black Moon/implication of the FAR: FUYUTSUKI: What is this? IKARI (OFF): A cavern left behind by someone, who was not us. Though eighty-nine percent of it is buried. FUYUTSUKI: An underground cavern that was originally a neat spherical shape... Is this the same as the underground cavern found at the South Pole?! IKARI (OFF): The data from them are virtually identical. FUYUTSUKI: Do you intend to repeat that tragedy once again?! Is that what you're doing?! IKARI: Whether we are or not, please determine that for yourself. All the resources mankind has to offer are being poured into that facility.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Apr 15, 2011 1:25 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: This was a rather jarring cut for me, but I guess it's supposed to be some sudden passage of time/jumping to Second Impact? Should the video footage of reflected that, or was somehow reflected and I just missed it?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Apr 16, 2011 12:16 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Gendo is the one who proposed HIP (and I guess by extension, 3I?) to Kiel/Seele, yes? So what's with this exchange between Fuyutsuki and Gendo?

[]:             Quote Post by DevRei17 » Sat Apr 16, 2011 12:02 pm I started a rewatch back in October and got to episode 15 before I was caught up with some other things. I've decided to continue today and just finished 16 & 17. Two quick thoughts: * I must be really rusty as I forgot all about the American branch disappearing! * I find the Shinji and Touji at Rei's house/Touji and Hikari's afterschool discussion adorable scenes.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Mon Sep 12, 2011 12:09 am Sorry to grave-dig this, but I wanted to find the proper thread instead of cluttering up the place with a new one... I was just watching 22' (English Dub), and got to the scene where Shinji, Rei, and Asuka are on the train platform. A few things: a) The man who announces the arrival of the train ("For your safety, please wait behind the yellow line...") sounds vaguely familiar. Is anyone able to recognize who he is? b) Artistic values of an "unbridgeable gap" aside, why did Asuka and Shinji/Rei get onto separate platforms? I can understand if Asuka had just gone down the platform to make a call, but going all the way across the tracks seems odd. c) "OK, so I lost this time. That doesn't change the fact that you're the one w- " [TRAIN ARRIVES] I'm surprised this "missing line" hasn't come up more in discussions surrounding, say, Gendo's more infamous one. I'm not entirely sure what got cut off, but it doesn't sound like the standard "curse cut short." Is it simply a swear, or something more meaningful? Commentary: I'm not sure if it's because it's the DC version, but I really like the animation in this episode. Aside from a short aberration where Asuka blows up on Shinji after her phone call, colors, eyes, and movements stay subdued, proportional, and smooth, respectively. And the infamous 51-second elevator scene has gone from one of my most-disliked scenes to one of my favorites.... Mostly because the first time I saw it, a "WTF?" reaction settled in quickly, and the only thing that kept me skipping it was a worry that I would accidentally go too far and miss something important. On repeated viewings, I've come to realize this is a masterpiece of tensioning and "giving the screws another twist," since it closely replicates the real discomfort of being on long elevator ride with someone you dislike. Asuka's twitch in the middle provides an immense relief for our own tension... But only does it so it can build the hackle-raising silence all over again. The bastards. :) And when Rei finally speaks, it's unexpected enough to make me jump a little.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Sep 12, 2011 1:57 am Fireand'chutes77 wrote: c) "OK, so I lost this time. That doesn't change the fact that you're the one w- " [TRAIN ARRIVES]

[]:             Quote Post by Razeil100 » Mon Sep 12, 2011 3:59 am Just finished my re-watch. (4th time I think? The show anyways, going to do EoE tomorrow). Its funny now that i'm older (first saw Eva in middle school, i'm now a freshman in University) the final episodes are so much easier to wrap my head around. When I first saw them, nothing really processed. I was just kinda like...WAT. Watching 25 & 26 now, I not only process everything, but I took a lot out of the message. As depressing as that show gets, by the end of 26 I was smiling, because it's really simple advice and so very true. Changing your disposition makes all the difference. Also, the amount of recycled footage in the law stretch of the show is crazy. I actually had a question on the ending too. Kaji is very clearly a part of Intrumentality/Complementation in EoTV, but I can't seem to remember him playing a part in EoE's version. He'd been dead for a few weeks at that point, so i'm not sure how he would have participated. I doubt Gendo anchored his soul to anything, wouldn't make sense or seem reasonable given his location when he gets shot. I like to think EoTV and EoE running parallel, with EoTV being a more indepth look into Instrumentality during EoE. This would make sense due to the images of Misato and Ritsuko and Asuka underwater in Unit 02 in 25 & 26, but then I come back to the issue of Kaji's participation. The only time I can remember Kaji in EoE is "Misato and Kaji's Week long sextravaganza", but I thought that could be Misato's memories being played out. Sorry if that dragged on or rambled, its late.

I believe at that point China would probably have been constructing a "proper" Eva. The MP Eva's were most likely all constructed in the same place, and didn't seem to be related to any country; just Seele itself. ~Whether an Angel would have made contact with the Adam embryo or contacting Lilith in Terminal Dogma, WOULD Third Impact have indeed occurred? Episode 19 says this is the case, but I guess I'm confused on the matter because neither Lilith or Adam are reunited with their respective souls until EoE... Rei's soul is split between Rei

Article: Random questions NGE Rewatch
OULD Third Impact have indeed occurred? Episode 19 says this is the case, but I guess I'm confused on the matter because neither Lilith or Adam are reunited with their respective souls until EoE... Rei's soul is split between Rei 2 and Eva-00, while Kaworu's apparently off in Germany (I guess?) somewhere, so what would the Angels have to contact, really? In Adam's case, perhaps merging with Adam causes the Angel to become Adam, but I'm not sure what would happen with Lilith except for Angel pokes Lilith, BOOM!, Life is reset/everybody's tanged. And maybe that is that's all there would be to it? The Eva2 game makes a point of Rei being drawn to Lilith (and merging with Lilith) if an Angel reaches Terminal Dogma, which is a nice touch, but... Again, who knows. The major problem here is that the answer itself probably changed several times throughout the series. In hindsight, it SEEMS that the only real way 3I can occur is if Lilith or Adam had their respective souls returned to them and if either one was touched by another angel, because that is what both Seele and Gendo attempted to do (and is ultimately what happened in a roundabout fashion). Any statements to the contrary throughout the series can then be chalked up to ignorance on the part of the character or deliberate misdirection. ~I'm sure this was asked before, but considering Rei's problems with Eva-00, why didn't Nerv HQ send for Asuka and Eva-02 sooner? Of course, Gendo needed Shinji inside Eva-01/Yui as part of his plans (pretty sure Eva-01 gaining the S2 Engine was on his Third Impact Checklist), but wouldn't it smarter for them to have more backup, especially considering Shinji was completely inexperienced whereas Rei and Asuka have had formal training with Eva? Plot-wise, it makes sense for Shinji being solo with piloting for awhile and in general him getting used to his surroundings, but in-universe-wise, it doesn't seem very smart on Nerv's part. The least they could have done was send for Shinji sooner to train him, but I guess Nerv had no idea when the Angels would appear versus not, unless the DSS say something about that. Who knows. They did transport Unit 02 an Asuka by ship from Germany, but that shouldn't take more than what, a couple weeks? Maybe

Article: Random questions NGE Rewatch
 idea when the Angels would appear versus not, unless the DSS say something about that. Who knows. They did transport Unit 02 an Asuka by ship from Germany, but that shouldn't take more than what, a couple weeks? Maybe 3? The timeline is sketchy between the first episode and when we see Asuka, but I'm 90% sure that Gendo says within the first couple episodes that Rei will be ready to fight in something like 22 days, and she is back up and running before Asuka arrives, so.... ~On a similar line of thought, wouldn't it have been better on the "Marduk Institute" (IT'S GENDO!) to find and train the Fourth Children ASAP? Of course, they'd need an Eva unit to go along with the Children, but why not work on a "backup" Eva/potential pilot on the off-chance an Angel really injures/screws over the active pilots? Well, without an Eva, there would have been no point. As far as we know, a 4th pilot wouldn't be able to operate any of the other Evas.

Article: Random questions NGE Rewatch
 who announces the arrival of the train ("For your safety, please wait behind the yellow line...") sounds vaguely familiar. Is anyone able to recognize who he is? b) Artistic values of an "unbridgeable gap" aside, why did Asuka and Shinji/Rei get onto separate platforms? That doesn't change the fact that you're the one w- " [TRAIN ARRIVES] I'm surprised this "missing line" hasn't come up more in discussions surrounding, say, Gendo's more infamous one. I'm not entirely sure what got cut off, but it doesn't sound like the standard "curse cut short." Is it simply a swear, or something more meaningful? Commentary: I'm not sure if it's because it's the DC version, but I really like the animation in this episode. Aside from a short aberration where Asuka blows up on Shinji after her phone call, colors, eyes, and movements stay subdued, proportional, and smooth, respectively. And the infamous 51-second elevator scene has gone from one of my most-disliked scenes to one of my favorites.... Mostly because the first time I saw it, a "WTF?" reaction settled in quickly, and the only thing that kept me skipping it was a worry that I would accidentally go too far and miss something important. On repeated viewings, I've come to realize this is a masterpiece of tensioning and "giving the screws another twist," since it closely replicates the real discomfort of being on long elevator ride with someone you dislike. Asuka's twitch in the middle provides an immense relief for our own tension... But only does it so it can build the hackle-raising silence all over again. The bastards. :) And when Rei finally speaks, it's unexpected enough to make me jump a little.

Its funny now that i'm older (first saw Eva in middle school, i'm now a freshman in University) the final episodes are so much easier to wrap my head around. When I first saw them, nothing really processed. I was just kinda like...WAT. Watching 25 & 26 now, I not only process everything, but I took a lot out of the message. As depressing as that show gets, by the end of 26 I was smiling, because it's really simple advice and so very true. Changing your disposition makes all the difference. Also, the amount of recycled footage in the law stretch of the show is crazy. I actually had a question on the ending too. Kaji is very clearly a part of Intrumentality/Complementation in EoTV, but I can't seem to remember him playing a part in EoE's version. He'd been dead for a few weeks at that point, so i'm not sure how he would have participated. I doubt Gendo anchored his soul to anything, wouldn't make sense or seem reasonable given his location when he gets shot. I like to think EoTV and EoE running parallel, with EoTV being a more indepth look into Instrumentality during EoE. This would make sense due to the images of Misato and Ritsuko and Asuka underwater in Unit 02 in 25 & 26, but then I come back to the issue of Kaji's participation. The only time I can remember Kaji in EoE is "Misato and Kaji's Week long sextravaganza", but I thought that could be Misato's memories being played out. Sorry if that dragged on or rambled, its late.
